Supplied and suppressed marks

Introduction

WLC, like the LC itself, has three dually-cantillated prose passages: the two Decalogues (Exodus 20, Deuteronomy 5) and the Reuben/Bilhah verse (Genesis 35:22). Before attempting to grammar-check one of these passages we detangle it into its two single-cantillation strands, guided by MAM’s strands. After detangling a passage, we feed each strand’s chanted verses to the ordinary prose accent grammar checker. The accents checked are WLC’s own, except for the cases described below.

Sometimes WLC leaves a word without an accent in one strand. In these cases, the detangler supplies that one accent from MAM, making that strand’s chanted verse grammatical. These charitably-supplied accents are recorded below. This is a third kind of charity, distinct from the two reinterpretations on the almost-errors page: those reinterpret the type of geresh present in WLC, whereas a supplied mark adds a mark not present in WLC.

A verse with a supplied mark parses cleanly, so it is listed here since it will not be listed among the ungrammatical verses. One verse, Deuteronomy 5:8, gets a supplied accent that fixes the taḥton strand, but the elyon strand’s accent is ungrammatical. Therefore, the beleaguered word of that verse is listed both here and among the ungrammatical verses.

Supplied accents

Exodus 20:3: the detangler supplies the taḥton’s merkha

WLC has only the elyon strand here: יהיֽה־ (ending in maqaf, carrying a meteg, with no accent of its own), so the detangler supplies the taḥton’s merkha from MAM: יהי֥ה.

Deuter 5:6: the detangler supplies the elyon’s tipeḥa

WLC has only the taḥton strand here: אנכי֙ (pashta), so the detangler supplies the elyon’s tipeḥa from MAM: אנכ֖י.

Deuter 5:6: the detangler supplies the elyon’s atnaḥ

WLC has only the taḥton strand here: אלה֔יך (zaqef qatan), so the detangler supplies the elyon’s atnaḥ from MAM: אלה֑יך.

Deuter 5:8: the detangler supplies the taḥton’s qadma

WLC has only a mis-transcription of the elyon strand here: תעש֥ה־ (ending in maqaf, carrying a merkha), so the detangler supplies the taḥton’s qadma from MAM: תעש֨ה. Unlike other supplied accents, there is support from the LC for this qadma, although the LC is in poor shape here, so the support is not unequivocal.

This word is further discussed here, among the ungrammatical verses.

Deuter 5:17: the detangler supplies the taḥton’s tipeḥa

WLC ends a chanted verse here in the elyon strand: תרצֽח׃ (silluq + sof pasuq), so the detangler supplies the taḥton’s mid-verse tipeḥa from MAM: תרצ֖ח.

Supplied and suppressed punctuation

In addition to suppressing (and sometimes supplying) accents, the detangler also suppresses and supplies three accent-coupled punctuation marks: maqaf, sof pasuq, and legarmeh. This punctuation work flows directly from the accent detangling. Indeed, these marks are so tightly coupled to their accents — or, in the case of maqaf, to the lack of an accent — that detangling accents and detangling punctuation can be regarded as one and the same activity.

The table below — sortable by clicking its header cells — lists every punctuation change the detangler makes, both suppressed and supplied.

Sorting by “Change” reveals that the Decalogues are largely punctuated according to the elyon: to correctly punctuate the elyon all that is needed is to suppress 8 punctuation marks that are taḥton-only. Correctly punctuating the taḥton, by contrast, takes much more work: 9 marks must be supplied and 24 elyon-only marks suppressed.

VerseStrandMarkChangeWord
G35:22pashutsof pasuqsuppliedישראֽל[׃]
E20:2taḥtonsof pasuqsuppressedעבד֑ים׃
E20:3taḥtonmaqafsuppliedלֽא[־]
E20:3taḥtonmaqafsuppressedיהי֥ה־
E20:3taḥtonsof pasuqsuppliedפנֽי[׃]
E20:4taḥtonlegarmehsuppressedפ֙סל֙׀
E20:4taḥtonlegarmehsuppressedבשמ֙ים֙׀
E20:4taḥtonlegarmehsuppressedבמ֖ים׀
E20:4taḥtonmaqafsuppliedלֽא[־]
E20:4taḥtonmaqafsuppressedתעש֨ה־
E20:4taḥtonsof pasuqsuppliedלאֽרץ[׃]
E20:8taḥtonsof pasuqsuppliedלקדשֽו[׃]
E20:9taḥtonsof pasuqsuppliedמלאכתֽך[׃]
E20:10taḥtonlegarmehsuppressedשב֖ת׀
E20:10taḥtonmaqafsuppressedובנך֣־
E20:10taḥtonsof pasuqsuppliedבשערֽיך[׃]
E20:13taḥtonsof pasuqsuppressedתרצ֖ח׃
E20:14taḥtonsof pasuqsuppressedתנא֑ף׃
E20:15taḥtonsof pasuqsuppressedתגנ֔ב׃
E20:5elyonsof pasuqsuppressedלשנא֑י׃
E20:10elyonmaqafsuppressedל֣א־
D5:6taḥtonsof pasuqsuppressedעבד֑ים׃
D5:7taḥtonmaqafsuppliedלא[־]
D5:7taḥtonmaqafsuppressedיהי֥ה־
D5:8taḥtonlegarmehsuppressedפ֙סל֙׀
D5:8taḥtonlegarmehsuppressedבשמ֙ים֙׀
D5:8taḥtonlegarmehsuppressedבמ֖ים׀
D5:8taḥtonmaqafsuppressedתעש֨ה־
D5:12taḥtonlegarmehsuppressedצוך֖׀
D5:12taḥtonsof pasuqsuppliedאלהֽיך[׃]
D5:14taḥtonlegarmehsuppressedשב֖ת׀
D5:15taḥtonlegarmehsuppressedהי֙ית֙׀
D5:15taḥtonmaqafsuppressedכ֣י־
D5:17taḥtonsof pasuqsuppressedתרצ֖ח׃
D5:18taḥtonsof pasuqsuppressedתנא֑ף׃
D5:19taḥtonsof pasuqsuppressedתגנ֔ב׃
D5:7elyonsof pasuqsuppressedפנ֗י׃
D5:8elyonmaqafsuppressedל֣א־
D5:8elyonsof pasuqsuppressedלא֗רץ׃
D5:9elyonsof pasuqsuppressedלשנא֑י׃
D5:13elyonsof pasuqsuppressedמלאכתך֒׃
D5:14elyonsof pasuqsuppressedכמ֑וך׃
